calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eduardo Sandoval for and with Eduardo Gutierrez. Ernest Jones, Jr. (Subscribing Witness) also present.

Mr. Sandoval explained circumstances and family history, and stated that the will was signed by Judge Victoire (now deceased) and Mr. Jones in 2005 and then given to Mr. Gutierrez for safe keeping. Mr. Sandoval also stated that the Decedent suffered an injury a year later, that she was at Laguna Honda Hospital from 2007 until her time of death in 2010, that she never returned home and that she had no access to the will. In addition, Mr. Sandoval spoke of expenses and creditors that were paid by Mr. Gutierrez. Mr. Gutierrez stated that his Sister (Decedent) never asked him to revoke the will. Mr. Jones stated that he witnessed the will being signed and that he stayed around while things were discussed. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Continued To Mar-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eduardo Sandoval for and with Eduardo Gutierrez. The Spouse of Mr. Gutierrez was also present.

The Court stated that it does not appear to be an original will and that it appears to be a copy of the will. The Court also informed Counsel that he will need to file a petition to probate a copy of the will, that he needs to re-notice and re-publish, and file a declaration that meets the issue of presumption that the will has not been destroyed. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Administration was continued to March 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
